author: @paprjam
text: I tried but failed. A rubberband helicopter that just flopped around but my granddaughter still laughed and laughed. Then we tried a battery and wire Homopolar motor and it just would not turn. She was disappointed and kept asking if we were still crafting??

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is a cool story - you should have submitted anyway - I didn't say the motor had to work!!

author: @ArjayMartin
text: Congratulations to the winners, As a suggestion, please make the entries close after a weekend and give final reminder before hand as silly buggers like me forget the dates but wanted to supervise the children this weekend for it, so others might have been in the same boat.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good suggestion mate - I will in future

author: @ArjayMartin
text: I just told my children about it today, and the bike one, but obviously forgot when it expired

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oops! lol

author: @martinsaint9999
text: We always imagine there is a lot of competition  but sometimes there simply isn't. We can learn something from this competitive situation. I have always observed that you can win something with better odds  if you have to be creative when you enter a contest (f.e. paint a picture). Quite a lot of folks are deterred by imaginary competition.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: honestly mate - the whole isn't about winning - it really is about taking part

author: @jazzrat
text:
A little feedback if its ok. I was keen to enter but the idea of doing a video was very daunting. I was too anxious to put myself in front of a camera and film.
Love the idea and so halpy that we have 2 winners. I can't wait to watch their videos.
Thank you for your amazing educational videos. I absolutely love your content

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you don't have to be in front of the camera mate - plenty of folks just show hands and a voice over

author: @RedPillRachel
text: I'm so disappointed about the lack of entries. Thanks anyway for trying to inspire people!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is a little but it is only the third competition we have run - these things can take a little time to get going!

replies.author: @RedPillRachel

author: @mrdewilliams
text:
I had several ideas, but finding a young person to do any of them with is the hard part.  My children are all adults, and I have no grandchildren yet.  Most of my family live in distant states, or in other countries.  People tend to be suspicious of an old man they don't know well that wants to borrow a child for making a video.
Don't be disheartened by the limited entries.  All the better chance for a (grand)parent/(grand)child to win the prize.

replies.author: @wayne975
replies.text: Ditto for me too.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice inmate - thanks for making those points

author: @zacharysimpson7353
text: I see the respect difference between alot of people talking to them.  People need to work together and not alone or there's no better future.  I'm smothering myself with information and people came to me I'd be able to give what people need in 1 or 10 years depending on the respect,  giving what you want is another time. I have to learn mass size organizations of products each location has on this planet aka foods, recycled goods, (building organizations for metals/glass/science products for something special for earth).  There's a better security system idea as in for everything we do and who deserves what they love to do.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @ThunderDivine
text: I planned to join this, but time escaped us.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it does have a nasty habit of doing that lol

author: @johnkay1336
text: Then you remove all the enamel from one arm , but only scrap 180 deg off the other arm. Hang in the hooks in front of the magnet and away she goes. Can be made in less than 90 seconds. With out using tools, magnet can scrap enamel off the wire.  Retired old valve and micro tech guy.  :-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers

author: @johnkay1336
text: enamelled wire is coiled, diameter 10mm with the ends wrapped twice around the bundle to hold the coil together. The two 20mm ends of the wire form the axel suspended in the hooks.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice

author: @johnkay1336
text: Sorry I missed out on this, I would have suggested, a steel jacket C cell, a magnet, two paper clips, and 10 inches of enamelled copper wire. The two paper clips are formed in to hooks and held to the C cell ends with one hand, the magnet sticks to the C cell wall and the

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good suggestion mate

author: @Runescape.
text: you give off real vibes of jimy saville mate.  you wanting to get around kids are ya,   well then.  we know what sort you are.   tisk tisk.    might as well close this channel down now before you get put in jail mate.  we know they're comin for ya, coppers gonna come for ya.

author: @mac-qt3wd
text: Hi Rob, I know magnetism is a bizarre subject and allegedly not fully understood but my question is about magnetic and non magnetic metals, maybe even specifically iron. You can have the same material ie iron ferrite attract or repel depending on a pole arrangement. But if you add electricity into say a gold coin ot can be attracted to a magnet and am guessing repelled from one too I dunno. Also is red Mercury a thing in your opinion? Read its in old tvs radios possibly and it attracts gold/non magnetic materials to it? Could it repel them too? Am thinking an electromagnet/red Mercury motor haha, Mercury and electricity/magnetism is worth a look at too imo

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: red mercury really does sound like a load of bunk mate - but that is just my opinion and I don't really know - but I would be trying to buy a kilo - it's around 900k per kilo I believe

replies.author: @mac-qt3wd
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I'm still not sure about red Mercury, if the powers that be deny its existence then its probably a real thing. I always thought Mercury was in thermometers, and they used to be red. The other people In charge say there's no such thing as free or zero point energy, I think we all know they're lying about that haha

author: @bugz000
text:
i'm a single dad and my lad is 4 i had no idea this competition was going on! he'd have absolutely loved this, only two entries tells me either youtube inhibited the distribution of the video for some reason (maybe kids involved, it's all bot controlled these days)  or a pure statistical anomaly! absolutely try again, a sample size of one is no metric of any result, so please please do make some more kids competitions, i'd absolutely love to get my boy involved, and don't be afraid to spam us busy mums and dads with a few reminder videos to ensure we all see! :D congrats to the lucky two that entered! 

i've also seen another channel (smartereveryday) set up a video reminder email service where you simply plug in your email address and you get told when videos come out via the email and given a link, very simple - perhaps a similar thing can be done for these competitions? something to consider if relying on a video release could be the main factor in people missing it - i'm quite dissapointed i missed this one i must say but i will remain positive that you will do more in future!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: this is only the third competition we have run mate - it does take a while for things to get known - cheers

author: @Milkybar3320011
text: Always comes down to time, don’t be disheartened, your a top bloke who brings great inspiration.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: wow - cheers mate

author: @karlmyers6518

I have to apologise for not taking the time. My little boy is 3 and I have been busy trying to keep up with the videos let alone make one. 

Well done to the winners and I'm looking forward to seeing your entry

replies.text: really it's no worries mate - bless your heart
